title: AOM Scenario Config
description: Data-only config for one agentic scenario. Adding a scenario = drop one JSON file here. No code in any of the four languages.

id.description: Scenario id; must match the filename (toll.json -> toll) and the data/<id>/ directory.

mode.description: single = one Agent A wallet, one settlement. race = N wallets contend for one resource, first-funded wins, losers refund.

wallets.description: Number of Agent A wallets. single uses 1; race uses >1.

resourceId.description: race only: the contested resource (ticketId/seatId). null in single mode.

amountMon.description: Escrow amount per lock, in MON (decimal string).

hook.description: One-line pitch shown on the idle screen.

agentA.description: Agent A display name (e.g. Car Wallet).

agentB.description: Agent B display name (e.g. Toll Oracle).

outcome.description: Short (3-5 word) outcome headline for the scenario card, e.g. 'Pay a toll, no app.'

agentBRole.description: Short specialist caption under Agent B on the card, e.g. 'best-execution specialist'.

icon.description: An emoji identifying the scenario on its card, e.g. '🚗'.

agentADesc.description: One-line description of Agent A's job, shown in its console panel header.

agentBDesc.description: One-line description of Agent B's job, shown in its console panel header.

label.description: Verb-phrase shown while computing (e.g. Verifying GPS passage proof...).

data.description: Free-form payload the chosen panel renders. Shape depends on panel type.

fields.description: Ordered field names forming the keccak256 proof recipe.

count.description: Autonomous-loop iterations (single mode). In race mode, one round of `wallets` locks.
